description: Read latest emails from Gmail inbox with metadata extraction (subject, sender, date, read status, starred status)
author: stillwater

reasoning.research: Gmail inbox renders email rows using role='row' ARIA elements. Each row contains subject (role='heading'), sender ([email] attribute), date, and read/starred indicators. Unread emails have class .zE (bold styling). Extraction is read-only and does not trigger bot detection.
reasoning.selector_strategy: Use ARIA role selectors for stability. Role='row' for email list, role='heading' for subject. Fallback to class-based selectors for date and snippet.
reasoning.anti_bot_notes: Read-only operation — minimal bot detection risk. Navigate with saved session to avoid login. Wait for div[role='main'] to confirm page loaded.
reasoning.performance: Expected run time: 5-8 seconds (navigation 3s + wait 2s + extraction 1s). No typing needed.

limit.description: Maximum number of emails to read. Default 10.

steps.description: Navigate to Gmail inbox and wait for main container
Verify we are authenticated — if redirected to login, abort with SESSION_EXPIRED
Wait for email rows to render in inbox
Extract email metadata from visible inbox rows
Return extracted emails as structured data

snippet.example: Just wanted to confirm our meeting...

error_handling.session_expired: Detect accounts.google.com redirect — abort with SESSION_EXPIRED error
error_handling.empty_inbox: Return empty emails array — not an error
error_handling.selector_timeout: Retry once with 15s timeout, then abort
